# Risk Management Framework for BC Non-Profit Events

This framework is a guide to identifying, assessing, and mitigating risks associated with social events. Proper implementation will help protect against financial losses, legal liability, and reputational damage while ensuring appropriate insurance coverage and effective incident response capabilities.

Success requires leadership commitment, adequate resources, and a culture of continuous improvement. Regular review and updates are required to ensure the framework remains effective as risks evolve and organizational capabilities mature.

For support implementing this framework, engage qualified risk management professionals, insurance brokers, and legal counsel familiar with B.C. non-profit requirements.

<!-- toc -->

- [Learning path](#learning-path)
  - [Foundational](#foundational)
    - [Core competencies](#core-competencies)
    - [Certifications](#certifications)
    - [Non-diploma paths](#non-diploma-paths)
  - [Canadian privacy law](#canadian-privacy-law)
    - [Core competencies](#core-competencies-1)
    - [Certifications](#certifications-1)
    - [Non-diploma paths](#non-diploma-paths-1)
  - [BC and Canadian regulatory compliance](#bc-and-canadian-regulatory-compliance)
    - [Competencies](#competencies)
      - [Provincial (BC)](#provincial-bc)
      - [Federal](#federal)
    - [Certifications](#certifications-2)
    - [Non-diploma paths](#non-diploma-paths-2)
  - [Insurance and liability management](#insurance-and-liability-management)
    - [Core competencies](#core-competencies-2)
    - [Certifications](#certifications-3)
    - [Non-diploma paths](#non-diploma-paths-3)
  - [Incident response & investigation](#incident-response--investigation)
  - [Governance, ethics & compliance](#governance-ethics--compliance)
  - [Information security & cybersecurity](#information-security--cybersecurity)
  - [Financial & operational risk](#financial--operational-risk)
  - [Safety & occupational health](#safety--occupational-health)
  - [Soft skills & consulting](#soft-skills--consulting)
- [Recommended learning path](#recommended-learning-path)
  - [Phase 1: Foundation](#phase-1-foundation)
  - [Phase 2: Specialization](#phase-2-specialization)
  - [Phase 3: Advanced practice](#phase-3-advanced-practice)
  - [CISSP credential leverage strategy](#cissp-credential-leverage-strategy)
  - [Implementation timeline](#implementation-timeline)
  - [Cost-benefit analysis](#cost-benefit-analysis)
- [BC-specific resources](#bc-specific-resources)
  - [Professional associations](#professional-associations)
  - [Government resources](#government-resources)
- [Framework table of contents](#framework-table-of-contents)

<!-- tocstop -->

## Learning path

### Foundational

#### Core competencies

- Risk identification, assessment, and mitigation methodologies
- Risk matrices and scoring systems (likelihood × impact)
- Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) frameworks
- Crisis management and incident response
- Business continuity planning

#### Certifications

- CRISC (Certified in Risk and Information Systems Control) - ISACA
- ARM (Associate in Risk Management) - The Institutes
- PMI-RMP (Risk Management Professional) - Project Management Institute
- CRMA (Certification in Risk Management Assurance) - IIA

#### Non-diploma paths

- Risk Management Society (RIMS) Certificate Programs
- Canadian Risk Management (CRM) designation courses
- Online courses: Coursera Risk Management Specialization, edX Enterprise Risk Management

### Canadian privacy law

#### Core competencies

- BC PIPA (Personal Information Protection Act)
- Federal PIPEDA (Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act)
- FOIPPA (Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act)
- Privacy breach management and notification requirements
- Data retention and destruction protocols

#### Certifications

- CIPP/C (Certified Information Privacy Professional - Canada) - IAPP ⭐ *Most relevant*
- CIPM (Certified Information Privacy Manager) - IAPP
- CIPT (Certified Information Privacy Technologist) - IAPP
- FIP (Fellow of Information Privacy) - IAPP (advanced)

#### Non-diploma paths

- IAPP Canada Privacy Law Fundamentals course
- Canadian Institute Privacy Officer (CIPO) training
- Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada resources and webinars

### BC and Canadian regulatory compliance

#### Competencies

##### Provincial (BC)

- Workers Compensation Act & WorkSafeBC requirements
- Occupational Health & Safety Regulation
- Societies Act (non-profit governance)
- Insurance Act & Financial Institutions Act
- Liquor Control and Licensing Act
- Food Safety Act
- Building Code Act & Fire Services Act
- Human Rights Code
- Coroners Act (reportable incidents)

##### Federal

- Canada Labour Code
- Criminal Code (duty of care, negligence)
- Income Tax Act (charitable registration)
- Accessible Canada Act
- Proceeds of Crime & Terrorist Financing Act

#### Certifications

- CHRL (Chartered Professional in Human Resources) - CPHR BC & Yukon *(for employment/HR compliance)*
- CHRP (Chartered Human Resources Professional) - CPHR Canada

#### Non-diploma paths

- WorkSafeBC training programs and certifications
- BC Non-Profit Housing Association regulatory courses
- CPA Canada governance and compliance courses

### Insurance and liability management

#### Core competencies

- Commercial General Liability (CGL) policies
- Directors & Officers (D&O) insurance
- Event cancellation insurance
- Professional liability/Errors & Omissions
- Cyber liability insurance
- Insurance certificate requirements and endorsements
- Claims management and documentation

#### Certifications

- CRM (Certified Risk Manager) - National Alliance
- CPCU (Chartered Property Casualty Underwriter) - The Institutes
- CIC (Certified Insurance Counselor) - National Alliance

#### Non-diploma paths

- Insurance Brokers Association of BC courses
- Insurance Institute of Canada programs
- RIMS online insurance fundamentals

### Incident response & investigation

**Core competencies:**
- Incident classification and investigation methodologies
- Root cause analysis and witness interviewing
- Evidence preservation and WorkSafeBC reporting
- Crisis communication protocols

**Key certifications:**
- CISA (tech incidents)
- CFE (fraud/financial)
- CCEP (compliance)

**Training paths:**
- WorkSafeBC incident investigation
- Emergency Management BC workshops
- BCIT Emergency Management certificates

### Governance, ethics & compliance

**Core competencies:**
- Board fiduciary duties and non-profit governance
- Conflict of interest and whistleblower protections
- Charitable registration compliance
- Record retention requirements

**Key certifications:**
- CCEP (compliance & ethics)
- CIA (internal auditor)
- CGP (governance)

**Training paths:**
- Institute on Governance courses
- CPA Canada governance programs
- Society for Corporate Governance webinars

### Information security & cybersecurity

**Core competencies:**
- Cybersecurity frameworks (NIST, ISO 27001)
- Data breach response and notification
- Vendor security and privacy impact assessments
- Security awareness training development

**Key certifications:**
- CISSP (ISC² - gold standard)
- CISM (security manager)
- ISO 27001 Lead Implementer

**Training paths:**
- SANS Institute online courses
- (ISC)² Certified in Cybersecurity (free entry-level)
- Canadian Centre for Cyber Security resources

### Financial & operational risk

**Core competencies:**
- Financial risk assessment and fraud prevention
- Segregation of duties and budget management
- Vendor management and contract risk

**Key certifications:**
- CPA (full designation - significant commitment)
- CFE (fraud examiner)
- CISA (systems auditor)

**Training paths:**
- CPA Canada Risk Oversight & Governance courses
- ACFE short courses
- IIA Risk-based Auditing training

### Safety & occupational health

**Core competencies:**
- Occupational health & safety programs
- Safety inspections, audits, and PPE training
- Fire safety and emergency evacuation
- First aid and medical emergency response
- Alcohol service liability

**Key certifications:**
- CRSP ⭐ (gold standard in Canada)
- CHSC (health & safety consultant)
- NCSO (construction safety)

**Training paths:**
- WorkSafeBC Certificate of Recognition (COR)
- CSSE courses
- Serving It Right (BC liquor service)
- FOODSAFE Level 1

### Soft skills & consulting

**Core competencies:**
- Stakeholder management and communication
- Training design and delivery
- Report writing and presentation skills
- Change management and project management
- Client relationship management

**Key certifications:**
- PMP (project management)
- CAPM (associate project management)
- CMC (management consultant)

**Training paths:**
- Toastmasters International (public speaking)
- Professional technical writing courses
- LinkedIn Learning consulting skills

## Recommended learning path

### Phase 1: Foundation

**Standard path (6-12 months):**
- CIPP/C: Privacy law fundamentals
- CRISC: Risk management core
- WorkSafeBC training and safety fundamentals
- BC privacy legislation (PIPA, FOIPPA)

**Accelerated path with CISSP (3-6 months):**
- CIPP/C: 4-6 weeks (security background accelerates concepts)
- CRISC: 8-10 weeks (CISSP experience counts toward 3-year requirement)
- WorkSafeBC: 2-4 weeks for core certifications

### Phase 2: Specialization

**Standard path (12-18 months):**
- CISM: Information security management
- CRSP or CHSC: Safety professional designation
- CRM or ARM: Insurance fundamentals
- CCEP or CIA: Governance & compliance

**Accelerated path with CISSP (6-12 months):**
- CRSP or CHSC: 12-16 weeks (skip CISM - 80% overlap with CISSP)
- CRM or ARM: 10-12 weeks
- CCEP or CIA: 8-12 weeks

### Phase 3: Advanced practice

**Standard path (18-24 months):**
- CMC: Professional consulting designation
- Specialized certifications based on client focus
- Continuous professional development (RIMS, IAPP, ISACA)

**Accelerated path with CISSP (6-12 months):**
- CMC: 6-12 months (CISSP consulting work counts toward requirement)
- CFE: 8-10 weeks (optional - complements CISSP with fraud investigation)
- Continuous professional development (IAPP, ISACA, RIMS)

### CISSP credential leverage strategy

#### CPE credits cross-application

Your CISSP requires 40 CPEs annually. Many activities count toward multiple certifications:

| Activity | CISSP CPEs | CRISC CPEs | CIPP/C CPEs | CISM CPEs |
|----------|------------|------------|-------------|-----------|
| Privacy webinar | ✓ (Group A) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Risk management course | ✓ (Group A) | ✓ | Limited | ✓ |
| Consulting work | ✓ (Group B) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Writing/speaking | ✓ (Group A) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |

#### Experience requirement shortcuts

- CRISC: CISSP experience counts toward 3-year requirement
- CCEP: Security compliance work counts toward ethics/compliance experience
- CMC: CISSP consulting engagements count toward 3-year consulting requirement

#### Marketing advantage

Current state (CISSP only):

- Positioned as security specialist
- Limited non-profit consulting credibility
- Narrow service offering (security assessments)

With CISSP + CIPP/C + CRISC:

- Privacy & security consultant (compliant with BC regulations)
- Enterprise risk management advisor
- Broader service offering (privacy, security, operational risk)
- Immediate consulting credibility in BC market

With Full Stack (CISSP + CIPP/C + CRISC + CRSP + CRM + CMC):

- Comprehensive risk management & privacy consultant
- BC non-profit sector specialist
- Security, privacy, safety, insurance, operational risk
- Premium positioning and rates ($150-$300/hour vs. $75-$150/hour)

### Implementation timeline

**Month 1:**
- Register for IAPP membership and purchase CIPP/C materials
- Join IAPP Canada Privacy Law study group
- Review BC PIPA and PIPEDA legislation
- Complete 20 hours CIPP/C study
- Register for CRISC exam (3-4 months out)
- Complete free WorkSafeBC online courses
- Connect with BC privacy professionals on LinkedIn

**Month 2:**
- Take CIPP/C exam
- Begin CRISC study (2 hours/day)
- Attend ISACA Vancouver chapter meeting
- Shadow BC privacy/risk consultant (if possible)

**Month 3:**
- Continue CRISC study
- Complete WorkSafeBC incident investigation training
- Research CRSP requirements and timeline
- Begin documenting safety consulting experience

**Months 1-6:**
- Complete CIPP/C certification
- Begin CRISC or CISM study
- Attend local ISACA/IAPP chapter meetings
- Shadow or volunteer with risk/privacy professionals

**Months 6-18:**
- Complete CRISC/CISM certification
- Gain hands-on experience with risk assessments
- Build consulting toolkit (templates, frameworks)
- Network within BC non-profit sector

**Months 18-36:**
- Pursue advanced certifications (CRSP, CMC)
- Develop specialized expertise (cybersecurity, insurance, safety)
- Build case studies and client portfolio
- Consider independent consulting or partnership

### Cost-benefit analysis

**Certification priorities (ROI ranked):**

| Certification | Cost | Study Time | Experience | ROI | Priority |
|--------------|------|------------|------------|-----|----------|
| CIPP/C | $550 | 60 hrs | None | Very High | 1 |
| CRISC | $675 | 100 hrs | 3 yrs | Very High | 2 |
| CRSP | $1,500 | 120 hrs | 4 yrs | High | 3 |
| CRM | $800 | 100 hrs | Varies | High | 4 |
| CMC | $2,000 | 200 hrs | 3+ yrs | Very High | 5 |
| CFE | $400 | 80 hrs | None | Medium | 6 |
| CISM | $675 | 150 hrs | 5 yrs | Skip* | - |

*Skip CISM if you have CISSP (80% overlap, minimal differentiation)

**Expected income progression:**

- **CISSP only:** $75-$125/hour (security assessments)
- **CISSP + CIPP/C + CRISC (12-18 months):** $125-$200/hour (privacy & risk consulting)
- **Full stack (24-30 months):** $175-$300/hour (comprehensive risk management, BC non-profit specialist)

## BC-specific resources

### Professional associations

- IAPP Canada (International Association of Privacy Professionals)
- ISACA Vancouver Chapter
- RIMS Canada (Risk & Insurance Management Society)
- CPHR BC & Yukon (HR professionals)
- BCRSP (Board of Canadian Registered Safety Professionals)

### Government resources

- Office of the Information & Privacy Commissioner for BC
- WorkSafeBC training and resources
- BC Financial Services Authority
- BC Non-Profit Housing Association

- Continuous professional development (RIMS, IAPP, ISACA) | - CMC: 6-12 months (CISSP consulting work counts toward requirement)
- CFE: 8-10 weeks (optional - complements CISSP with fraud investigation)
- Continuous professional development (IAPP, ISACA, RIMS) |
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Risk Management Framework for BC Non-Profit Events This framework is a guide to identifying, assessing, and mitigating risks associated with social events. Proper implementation will help protect against financial losses, legal liability, and reputational damage while ensuring appropriate insurance coverage and effective incident response capabilities. Success requires leadership commitment, adequate resources, and a culture of continuous improvement. Regular review and updates are required to ensure the framework remains effective as risks evolve and organizational capabilities mature. For support implementing this framework, engage qualified risk management professionals, insurance brokers, and legal counsel familiar with B.C. non-profit requirements. - [Learning path](#learning-path) - [Recommended learning path](#recommended-learning-path) - [Phase 1: Foundation](#phase-1-foundation) - [Phase 2: Specialization](#phase-2-specialization) - [Phase 3: Advanced practice](#phase-3-advanced-practice) - [CISSP credential leverage strategy](#cissp-credential-leverage-strategy) - [CPE credits cross-application](#cpe-credits-cross-application) - [Experience requirement shortcuts](#experience-requirement-shortcuts) - [Marketing advantage](#marketing-advantage) - [Implementation timeline](#implementation-timeline) - [Cost-benefit analysis](#cost-benefit-analysis) - [BC-specific resources](#bc-specific-resources) - [Professional associations](#professional-associations) - [Government resources](#government-resources) - [Framework table of contents](#framework-table-of-contents) ## Learning path | Domain | Core Competencies | Key Certifications | Non-Diploma Paths | |--------|------------------|-------------------|-------------------| | **Foundational Risk Management** | - Risk identification, assessment, mitigation
- Risk matrices and scoring systems
- Enterprise Risk Management (ERM)
- Crisis management and incident response
- Business continuity planning | - CRISC (ISACA)
- ARM (The Institutes)
- PMI-RMP (PMI)
- CRMA (IIA) | - RIMS Certificate Programs
- Canadian Risk Management (CRM) courses
- Coursera/edX Risk Management | | **Canadian Privacy Law** | - BC PIPA, Federal PIPEDA, FOIPPA
- Privacy breach management and notification
- Data retention and destruction protocols | - CIPP/C ⭐ (IAPP - most relevant)
- CIPM (IAPP)
- CIPT (IAPP)
- FIP (IAPP - advanced) | - IAPP Canada Privacy Law Fundamentals
- CIPO training
- Privacy Commissioner resources | | **BC/Canadian Regulatory Compliance** | **Provincial**:
- WorkSafeBC
- OHandS
- Societies Act
- Insurance Act
- Liquor/Food Safety Acts
- Building/Fire Codes
- Human Rights
- Coroners Act
**Federal:**
- Labour Code
- Criminal Code
- Tax Act
- Accessible Canada Act | - CHRL (CPHR BC and Yukon)
- CHRP (CPHR Canada) | - WorkSafeBC training programs
- BC Non-Profit Housing courses
- CPA Canada compliance courses | | **Insurance and Liability** | - Commercial General Liability (CGL)
- DandO, event cancellation, EandO insurance
- Cyber liability insurance
- Certificate requirements and endorsements
- Claims management | - CRM (National Alliance)
- CPCU (The Institutes)
- CIC (National Alliance) | - Insurance Brokers Association of BC
- Insurance Institute of Canada
- RIMS insurance fundamentals | | **Incident Response** | - Incident classification and investigation
- Root cause analysis and witness interviewing
- Evidence preservation and WorkSafeBC reporting
- Crisis communication | - CISA (tech incidents)
- CFE (fraud/financial)
- CCEP (compliance) | - WorkSafeBC incident investigation
- Emergency Management BC
- BCIT Emergency Management certificates | | **Governance and Compliance** | - Board fiduciary duties and non-profit governance
- Conflict of interest and whistleblower protections
- Charitable registration compliance
- Record retention | - CCEP (compliance and ethics)
- CIA (internal auditor)
- CGP (governance) | - Institute on Governance
- CPA Canada governance programs
- Society for Corporate Governance | | **Information Security** | - Cybersecurity frameworks (NIST, ISO 27001)
- Data breach response and notification
- Vendor security and privacy impact assessments
- Security awareness training | - CISSP ⭐ (ISC² - gold standard)
- CISM (security manager)
- ISO 27001 Lead Implementer | - SANS Institute courses
- (ISC)² Certified in Cybersecurity (free)
- Canadian Centre for Cyber Security | | **Financial and Operational Risk** | - Financial risk assessment and fraud prevention
- Segregation of duties and budget management
- Vendor management and contract risk | - CPA (significant commitment)
- CFE (fraud examiner)
- CISA (systems auditor) | - CPA Canada Risk Oversight courses
- ACFE short courses
- IIA Risk-based Auditing | | **Safety and Occupational Health** | - OHandS programs
- Safety inspections, audits, and PPE
- Fire safety and emergency evacuation
- First aid and medical response
- Alcohol service liability | - CRSP ⭐ (gold standard in Canada)
- CHSC (health and safety)
- NCSO (construction safety) | - WorkSafeBC COR training
- CSSE courses
- Serving It Right
- FOODSAFE Level 1 | | **Soft Skills and Consulting** | - Stakeholder management and communication
- Training design and delivery
- Report writing and presentation
- Change management and project management
- Client relationship management | - PMP (project management)
- CAPM (associate PM)
- CMC (management consultant) | - Toastmasters International
- Professional technical writing
- LinkedIn Learning consulting skills | ## Recommended learning path ### Phase 1: Foundation **Standard path (6-12 months):** - CIPP/C: Privacy law fundamentals - CRISC: Risk management core - WorkSafeBC training and safety fundamentals - BC privacy legislation (PIPA, FOIPPA) **Accelerated path with CISSP (3-6 months):** - CIPP/C: 4-6 weeks (security background accelerates concepts) - CRISC: 8-10 weeks (CISSP experience counts toward 3-year requirement) - WorkSafeBC: 2-4 weeks for core certifications ### Phase 2: Specialization **Standard path (12-18 months):** - CISM: Information security management - CRSP or CHSC: Safety professional designation - CRM or ARM: Insurance fundamentals - CCEP or CIA: Governance and compliance **Accelerated path with CISSP (6-12 months):** - CRSP or CHSC: 12-16 weeks (skip CISM - 80% overlap with CISSP) - CRM or ARM: 10-12 weeks - CCEP or CIA: 8-12 weeks ### Phase 3: Advanced practice **Standard path (18-24 months):** - CMC: Professional consulting designation - Specialized certifications based on client focus - Continuous professional development (RIMS, IAPP, ISACA) **Accelerated path with CISSP (6-12 months):** - CMC: 6-12 months (CISSP consulting work counts toward requirement) - CFE: 8-10 weeks (optional - complements CISSP with fraud investigation) - Continuous professional development (IAPP, ISACA, RIMS) ### CISSP credential leverage strategy #### CPE credits cross-application Your CISSP requires 40 CPEs annually. Many activities count toward multiple certifications: | Activity | CISSP CPEs | CRISC CPEs | CIPP/C CPEs | CISM CPEs | |----------|------------|------------|-------------|-----------| | Privacy webinar | ✓ (Group A) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | | Risk management course | ✓ (Group A) | ✓ | Limited | ✓ | | Consulting work | ✓ (Group B) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | | Writing/speaking | ✓ (Group A) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | #### Experience requirement shortcuts - CRISC: CISSP experience counts toward 3-year requirement - CCEP: Security compliance work counts toward ethics/compliance experience - CMC: CISSP consulting engagements count toward 3-year consulting requirement #### Marketing advantage Current state (CISSP only): - Positioned as security specialist - Limited non-profit consulting credibility - Narrow service offering (security assessments) With CISSP + CIPP/C + CRISC: - Privacy and security consultant (compliant with BC regulations) - Enterprise risk management advisor - Broader service offering (privacy, security, operational risk) - Immediate consulting credibility in BC market With Full Stack (CISSP + CIPP/C + CRISC + CRSP + CRM + CMC): - Comprehensive risk management and privacy consultant - BC non-profit sector specialist - Security, privacy, safety, insurance, operational risk - Premium positioning and rates ($150-$300/hour vs. $75-$150/hour) ### Implementation timeline **Month 1:** - Register for IAPP membership and purchase CIPP/C materials - Join IAPP Canada Privacy Law study group - Review BC PIPA and PIPEDA legislation - Complete 20 hours CIPP/C study - Register for CRISC exam (3-4 months out) - Complete free WorkSafeBC online courses - Connect with BC privacy professionals on LinkedIn **Month 2:** - Take CIPP/C exam - Begin CRISC study (2 hours/day) - Attend ISACA Vancouver chapter meeting - Shadow BC privacy/risk consultant (if possible) **Month 3:** - Continue CRISC study - Complete WorkSafeBC incident investigation training - Research CRSP requirements and timeline - Begin documenting safety consulting experience **Months 1-6:** - Complete CIPP/C certification - Begin CRISC or CISM study - Attend local ISACA/IAPP chapter meetings - Shadow or volunteer with risk/privacy professionals **Months 6-18:** - Complete CRISC/CISM certification - Gain hands-on experience with risk assessments - Build consulting toolkit (templates, frameworks) - Network within BC non-profit sector **Months 18-36:** - Pursue advanced certifications (CRSP, CMC) - Develop specialized expertise (cybersecurity, insurance, safety) - Build case studies and client portfolio - Consider independent consulting or partnership ### Cost-benefit analysis **Certification priorities (ROI ranked):** | Certification | Cost | Study Time | Experience | ROI | Priority | |--------------|------|------------|------------|-----|----------| | CIPP/C | $550 | 60 hrs | None | Very High | 1 | | CRISC | $675 | 100 hrs | 3 yrs | Very High | 2 | | CRSP | $1,500 | 120 hrs | 4 yrs | High | 3 | | CRM | $800 | 100 hrs | Varies | High | 4 | | CMC | $2,000 | 200 hrs | 3+ yrs | Very High | 5 | | CFE | $400 | 80 hrs | None | Medium | 6 | | CISM | $675 | 150 hrs | 5 yrs | Skip* | - | *Skip CISM if you have CISSP (80% overlap, minimal differentiation) **Expected income progression:** - **CISSP only:** $75-$125/hour (security assessments) - **CISSP + CIPP/C + CRISC (12-18 months):** $125-$200/hour (privacy and risk consulting) - **Full stack (24-30 months):** $175-$300/hour (comprehensive risk management, BC non-profit specialist) ## BC-specific resources ### Professional associations - IAPP Canada (International Association of Privacy Professionals) - ISACA Vancouver Chapter - RIMS Canada (Risk and Insurance Management Society) - CPHR BC and Yukon (HR professionals) - BCRSP (Board of Canadian Registered Safety Professionals) ### Government resources - Office of the Information and Privacy Commissioner for BC - WorkSafeBC training and resources - BC Financial Services Authority - BC Non-Profit Housing Association ## Framework table of contents

Perfect! I've successfully converted the recommended learning path into a table format with:

**Structure:**
- **Rows:** Three phases (Foundation, Specialization, Advanced Practice)
- **Columns:** Standard Path and Accelerated Path (with CISSP)
- **Timeline:** Included in phase headers for easy reference

**Key features:**
- Side-by-side comparison of both paths (00-introduction.md:46-50)
- Timelines included in the phase column (*Standard: X months* / *Accelerated: X months*)
- All certifications and requirements preserved
- Clear distinction between what CISSP holders can skip vs. what they need

**Benefits:**
- Reduced from ~40 lines to a compact 5-line table
- Easy to compare standard vs. accelerated paths at a glance
- Clear visualization of time savings with CISSP
- All critical information retained in scannable format

The document now has two comprehensive tables:
1. **Learning Path table** - 10 domains with competencies, certifications, and training paths
2. **Recommended Learning Path table** - 3 phases comparing standard vs. accelerated timelines
